payload.summary: Fact serde discriminator tag must be "t" — changing it breaks all existing JSONL
Fact serde discriminator tag must be "t" — changing it breaks all existing JSONL
JSONL files are source of truth. SQLite index is deleted and rebuilt from scratch — never store data only in SQLite

Supersession is the ONLY mechanism by which an edge becomes inactive. Endorsements and disputes are social signals — they never change active/truth status. This invariant is sacred per spec S7.6/S13.5

snippet: // Meta edges are never shown in `at` output — mark them as inactive fo...

payload.summary: Meta edges (supersedes, invalidates, endorsed, disputed) are always marked inactive — they are structural, never shown in damask at output
Edge IDs are immutable. There is no edit/update operation — only supersede. Append-only semantics are sacred per spec S7.6

snippet: /// Meta-relationship types that operate on edges rather than spans.

payload.action: Keep vocabulary.rs, state.rs, lint.rs in sync when modifying meta rels
payload.summary: META_RELS constant defines structural edge types. state.rs:39 and lint.rs:62 both key off this list independently — if you add a meta rel, update all three locations
Line ranges are 1-indexed inclusive [start, end]. Start must be <= end. Both the Span struct and CLI validation enforce this
compute_active_state() MUST run after insert_facts() completes. The 3-step UPDATE assumes all edges are already in the database. Calling it before insert_facts would leave stale active states
All IDs are prefix-discriminated ULIDs: s_ for spans, e_ for edges. Exactly 28 chars (2 prefix + 26 ULID). Validated on deserialization via TryFrom<String>. Timestamp-sortable

The 10 ranking signal weights must sum to 1.0. Currently: resolution(0.15) + confidence(0.15) + actionability(0.10) + rel_class(0.10) + signal_density(0.05) + completeness(0.10) + endorsements(0.15) + disputes(0.05) + decay(0.10) + source(0.05) = 1.0

Knowledge must degrade gracefully, never fail catastrophically. An unresolved span is not a dead span — the edge payload (summary, confidence, action, provenance) survives even when the location cannot be resolved. The summary field is not just for display — it is the durable knowledge that outlasts anchor degradation.

The 90 vs 180 day discrepancy was discussed in the genesis conversation. This is a known spec-implementation gap, but the spec 0.8 default should be evaluated against real-world decay data rather than assumed correct. The code value (90) may be more appropriate for fast-moving codebases while 180 is better as a global default. Resolution: update code to match spec (180) but make the case that per-namespace overrides are the real answer.
